heading: Transfer of title and possession
label: 3763 Transfer of title and possession
notes: CodificationSection is based on section 814 of title VIII of S. 2281, One Hundred Third Congress, as reported , which was enacted into law by .July 13, 1994Pub. L. 103–327

children.heading: Delivery of deeds
children.label: a Delivery of deeds
children.text: The foreclosure commissioner shall, upon delivery of a deed or deeds to the purchaser or purchasers (which shall be without warranty or covenants to the purchaser or purchasers) obtain the balance of the purchase price in accordance with the terms of sale provided in the notice of default and foreclosure sale. Notwithstanding any State law to the contrary, delivery of a deed by the foreclosure commissioner shall be a conveyance of the property, and constitute passage of title to the mortgaged property, and no judicial proceedings shall be required ancillary or supplementary to the procedures provided in this chapter to assure the validity of the conveyance or confirmation of such conveyance.

children.heading: Right of possession
children.label: b Right of possession
children.text: A purchaser at a foreclosure sale held pursuant to this chapter shall be entitled to possession upon passage of title under subsection (a) to the mortgaged property, subject to any interest or interests not barred under . Any person remaining in possession of the mortgaged property after the passage of title shall be deemed a tenant at sufferance subject to eviction under local law.section 3765 of this title

children.heading: Death of purchaser
children.label: c Death of purchaser
children.text: If a purchaser dies before execution and delivery of the deed conveying the property to the purchaser, the foreclosure commissioner shall execute and deliver the deed to a representative of the decedent purchaser’s estate upon payment of the purchase price in accordance with the terms of sale. Such delivery to the representative of the purchaser’s estate shall have the same effect as if accomplished during the lifetime of the purchaser.

children.heading: Bona fide purchaser
children.label: d Bona fide purchaser
children.text: The purchaser of property under this chapter shall be presumed to be a bona fide purchaser.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be no right of redemption, or right of possession based upon a right of redemption, in the mortgagor or others subsequent to a foreclosure completed pursuant to this chapter.

children.heading: Certain provisions
children.label: 2 Certain provisions
children.text: Section 1710() of this title and shall not apply to mortgages foreclosed under this chapter.lsection 1452c of title 42

children.heading: Taxes
children.label: f Taxes
children.text: When a mortgage foreclosed pursuant to this chapter is conveyed to the Secretary, no tax shall be imposed or collected with respect to the foreclosure commissioner’s deed (including any tax customarily imposed upon the deed instrument or upon the conveyance or transfer of title to the property). Failure to collect or pay a tax of the type and under the circumstances stated in the preceding sentence shall not be grounds for refusing to record such a deed, for failing to recognize such recordation as imparting notice, or for denying the enforcement of such a deed and its provisions in any State or Federal court.
